description_long: This chest of drawers features an ornate, classical design with a highly detailed relief on its front face, including two cherubic figures flanking a central shield adorned with a crown and laurel wreath. The shield itself displays a series of horizontal bars, possibly representing a coat of arms or heraldic symbol. The surface is richly textured, with intricate carvings and embellishments that suggest a baroque or renaissance influence. The sides of the object are relatively plain, emphasizing the depth and three-dimensionality of the front relief. The material appears to be a light-colored stone or plaster, giving it a stately and antique appearance. There are no visible handles or functional drawer separations, and the object seems more decorative than practical.
description: An ornate, carved chest of drawers with cherubs and a crowned shield relief, made from light-colored stone or plaster. The front is richly detailed, while the sides are plain.
description_view_0: Front view showing the elaborate relief with cherubs, a crowned shield, and laurel wreath.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the significant depth of the relief and the plain, unadorned side surface.
description_view_2: Another frontal view, similar to view 0, highlighting the detailed carvings and central shield.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the depth of the relief and the plain side profile.
